extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r4 (priority 50), which fires when diabetes AND renal_impairment AND elderly. rec_drug_b is set true only by r2 (priority 50), which fires when diabetes. With diabetes=true, renal_impairment=true, elderly=true, both r4 and r2 fire. r4 is the sole rule writing rec_drug_a and r2 is the sole rule writing rec_drug_b, so there is no competing rule to override either output via priority or order, and defaults only apply to unset outputs. Therefore rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true simultaneously, violating the mutual-exclusion property (rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b). seizure_history and low_ejection_fraction only affect rec_drug_c and are irrelevant; set to false in the witness. The conjunction in r4's guard already implies diabetes, so the conditions for both outputs are jointly satisfiable.
